bead.title: Score cheapest sufficient routes using effective cost
bead.description:
Update routing score semantics so policy decisions use comparable effective cost instead of treating subscription-backed models as equally included.

Desired scoring behavior:
- cheap selects the lowest effective-cost model with enough expected capability for the request. It should choose nano/mini/local over GPT-5.5 when they are sufficient and dispatchable.
- default selects the best routine tradeoff and should avoid maximum-quality frontier subscription unless lower-cost routine candidates are not sufficient or are materially worse after reliability/health/context scoring.
- smart can let quality/reliability dominate cost.
- Pay-per-token providers are hard-gated only when actual_cash_spend=true and metered opt-in is false.
- Subscription candidates use PAYG-equivalent effective cost for scoring while actual_cash_spend=false.
- Avoid frontier-specific if/else exclusion gates; the outcome should fall out of effective cost, power/task fit, reliability, latency, utilization, and risk scoring.

In scope: internal/routing scorer, service projection of snapshot effective_cost into routing candidates, focused score tests.

Out of scope: snapshot schema fields if prior bead has not landed; CLI display.
bead.acceptance:
1. go test ./internal/routing/... . -run 'Test.*Cheap|Test.*Default|Test.*Smart|Test.*EffectiveCost|Test.*Metered' -count=1 passes.
2. A cheap-policy test with available gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4-mini/nano, and a local/fixed candidate selects the lowest effective-cost sufficient candidate and not gpt-5.5.
3. A default-policy test selects a lower effective-cost routine candidate over gpt-5.5 when both satisfy the task.
4. A smart-policy test can select gpt-5.5 when quality/reliability score justifies it.
5. A metered test proves actual_cash_spend=true candidates are rejected without opt-in and can win with opt-in.
6. rg -n 'frontier_excluded_by_cheap|if .*gpt-5.5|if .*frontier' internal/routing service_routing.go returns no new policy-specific exclusion gate.

events.body: {"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: policy \"air-gapped\" has no candidate satisfying local endpoint: 5 candidates rejected"}
